summary: Wave 1/5: build the core editing foundation (undo/redo, multi-select+groups, copy/paste, align) + cheap polish (autosave indicator, first-run, grip affordance, juice, focus ring) for Brick City Planner, with expert implementer→review→fix loops
logs: Wave 1: final integration + full test pass...

review.testSummary: npm test: 46 pass / 0 fail (40 prior + 6 new — 5 pure-geometry tests for tileAABB/tilesInRect/alignTiles/distributeTiles/rotateGroup, plus 1 createGrid integration test driving place/undo/redo/group/align/copy/paste/duplicate/delete against a DOM mock). node --check --input-type=module OK on js/grid.js, js/geometry.js, js/app.js. New tests assert concrete values (non-vacuous).
review.specCoverage: All 4 spec items fully implemented and wired. QOL-1 undo/redo: 60-step snapshot stack with baseline-at-0, finalize() commits on every mutating action, Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z / Ctrl+Y, disabled-aware toolbar buttons + 🕘 history dropdown with jump-to-state. QOL-2 marquee+groups: Set-based selection keeping selectedId primary, rubber-band on empty canvas, Shift/Ctrl toggle, combined sel-box, group drag/rotate(about bbox centre)/delete/nudge, Ctrl+G/Ctrl+Shift+G, groupId persists through save/export. QOL-3 copy/paste/duplicate/alt-drag with id+groupId remap so copies are independent and become the new selection. QOL-4 align/distribute via pure geometry helpers on rotated AABBs, floating toolbar. No regressions: single-tile drag still snapConnects (own selection excluded), rotate handle/resize grip/layers/catalog drag-drop/save-restore intact. Perf in-place-update rule honored on all pointermove paths; render() only on structural changes. Pop keyframe still opacity-only. No new unescaped innerHTML (history labels via textContent, tile labels via esc()).

findings.issue: The editor-wide window keydown handler calls ev.preventDefault() for Ctrl+C/Ctrl+V/Ctrl+D/Ctrl+A whenever the target is not an input/textarea, even when nothing is selected or the clipboard is empty (copySelection/paste/duplicate return early but the handler still prevents default). This swallows the browser's native copy and select-all everywhere on the page outside form fields (e.g. selecting/copying the budget or set-list text in the summary panel).
findings.fix: In js/grid.js window keydown handler, only preventDefault when the app actually consumed the shortcut — e.g. gate on a return value: `if (k==='c'){ if (copySelection()) ev.preventDefault(); }` and similarly guard 'v'/'d'/'a' on selection/clipboard being non-empty.

findings.issue: The floating align bar appears at 2+ selected and shows the distribute-horizontally/vertically buttons, but distributeSelection() requires 3+ tiles and silently no-ops with exactly 2 selected. The buttons look active yet do nothing.
findings.fix: Disable (or hide) the two data-dist buttons when fewer than 3 tiles are selected — e.g. in app.js drawSelection, toggle a disabled attribute on [data-dist] based on ids.length < 3.

findings.issue: Nudge coalescing folds across intervening non-committing selection changes: nudging tile A, then clicking to select tile B (no commit), then nudging B merges both nudge runs into a single undo step because coalesceKey stays 'nudge'. State stays consistent but undo granularity is coarser than a user may expect.
findings.fix: Reset coalesceKey to null inside the selection mutators (select/selectIds/toggleSelect/refreshSelectionUI) so a selection change breaks the nudge coalescing chain; optional polish, not a correctness issue.

review.testSummary: `node --check --input-type=module` passes clean on js/app.js, js/grid.js, js/geometry.js. `npm test` → 46/46 pass (0 fail), including the 6 new tests (tileAABB, tilesInRect, alignTiles, distributeTiles, rotateGroup in test/geometry.test.mjs, plus the new integration suite test/grid.test.mjs covering undo/redo/group/align/copy-paste/duplicate/delete). No vacuous assertions found — every new test asserts concrete values/counts.
review.specCoverage: QOL-9 (autosave pill): implemented correctly — 'Saving…'/'All changes saved' driven by the debounce in app.js, wired through every finalize()/onChange() path including undo/redo/import, doesn't spam during in-progress drags. ACC-3 (focus ring + AA sizing): implemented well — global :focus-visible ring plus a tile-specific box-shadow halo (correctly reasoned to survive .tile's overflow:hidden), plus consistent font/icon-size bumps across catalog rows, summary stats, and toolbar icons; darker --warn/--ink-faint tokens for contrast. MOTION-6 (interaction juice): mostly implemented — overshoot pop-in on placement done without the fill:both rotation-override gotcha (verified: no fill-mode, custom --rot property, correctly reverts to inline transform), fade+scale delete ghosts are cosmetic/decoupled from the synchronous delete, drag-lift and marching-ants marquee/selection outline are done via non-transform techniques, everything is reduced-motion guarded — but the new transform transition on .tile is not suppressed during the free-rotate handle drag (only during position drags), see finding below. UI-2 (grip affordance + shortcuts modal): implemented — tooltip text updated, one-time intro pulse gated correctly, '?' key + toolbar button open an accurate, real shortcut list matching the actual undo/redo/copy/paste/group/nudge bindings in grid.js. UI-1 (first-run empty state): tip UI, sample-city loader, and auto-hide-when-non-empty all work, but the specified 'gentle pulsing highlight on #catalog-search' never renders (see finding below), and the 'don't show again this session' guarantee isn't fully honored if the board is emptied back out.

findings.issue: UI-1's 'gentle pulsing highlight on #catalog-search' never actually renders. js/app.js's updateFirstRun() does `$('catalog-search')?.classList.toggle('pulse', show)`, which toggles the `pulse` class on the `<input id="catalog-search">` element itself. But the CSS rule is `.search.pulse{animation:search-pulse ...}` (css/styles.css ~line 117), which only matches an element that has BOTH the `search` class AND the `pulse` class on itself. The `search` class lives on the *wrapping* `<div class="search">` (index.html line 41), not on the input — so the selector never matches anything and the animation silently never fires. Even if it did match the input, `.search input{border:0;...}` has no border/padding of its own to nicely display a ring around, confirming the wrapper div was the intended target.
findings.fix: Target the wrapper, not the input: in js/app.js change `$('catalog-search')?.classList.toggle('pulse', show)` to `$('catalog-search')?.closest('.search')?.classList.toggle('pulse', show)` (or give the wrapping `<div class="search">` its own id and toggle that). Then re-verify the CSS rule `.search.pulse{...}` actually matches.

findings.issue: The new tile transform transition (`.tile{transition:transform .16s cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1) ...}`, css/styles.css) is only suppressed during position-drags (via the `.dragging` class added in grid.js's tile pointerdown 'move' handler) — it is NOT suppressed during the free-rotate handle drag (`ev.target.classList.contains('rotate-handle')` branch in js/grid.js), whose `rot(e)` handler calls `applyRot(t)` on every pointermove, setting `el.style.transform` directly. That means every 15° increment during a live rotate-drag now goes through a 160ms elastic/overshoot easing curve instead of updating instantly, so continuous free-rotate is likely to feel laggy/rubbery and lag behind the pointer rather than track it 1:1 — a regression to feel/quality on an existing core interaction the spec explicitly said must not be broken (MOTION-6 only asked for a tween on discrete 'rotation snaps', i.e. R-key/90° presses, not continuous drag).
findings.fix: Add a class (e.g. `.tile.rotating{transition:none}`) mirroring the existing `.dragging` pattern: apply it to the tile at the start of the rotate-handle drag in js/grid.js's `rot`/`rend` handlers and remove it in `rend()` (mirroring how `dragging` is added/removed around the position-drag `move`/`end`). That keeps the tween for the deliberate R-key/90° single-step rotation (where it's wanted) while restoring instant 1:1 tracking during the free-rotate gesture.

findings.issue: UI-1's 'auto-dismiss... don't show again this session' isn't fully honored. `dismissFirstRun()` (which sets the `bcp.firstRunSeen` sessionStorage flag) is only called from the ✕ close button and 'Load a sample city' button. When a tile is placed the normal way (catalog click/drag-drop), `updateFirstRun()` merely computes `show = empty && !seen` reactively — it never sets the seen flag. So if the user places one tile then deletes it (board goes back to 0 tiles), the tip and search-pulse both reappear later in the same session, contradicting the literal spec wording.
findings.fix: In app.js's onChange-driven refresh path (or directly in updateFirstRun()), call the sessionStorage-setting half of dismissFirstRun() the first time placed.length transitions from 0 to >0, e.g. `if (!empty) { try { sessionStorage.setItem(FIRST_RUN_KEY,'1'); } catch {} }` before computing `show`, so it stays dismissed even if the board is later emptied.

findings.issue: `.align-bar{position:absolute;top:106px;...}` (css/styles.css) is a hardcoded pixel offset assuming a fixed single-line height for `.canvas-tools`. Today's bundle adds a 3-button `.hist-group` plus a `.tool-sep` plus the new `#btn-help` to that already-`flex-wrap:wrap` toolbar, making it noticeably longer and more likely to wrap to two lines on medium-narrow viewports (there's no toolbar-specific breakpoint, only the `@media (max-width:940px)` single-column layout switch). If it wraps, the align/distribute/group bar (shown whenever 2+ tiles are selected) would overlap the second toolbar row instead of sitting cleanly below it. Not confirmed in a real browser — flagging for a quick visual check at ~600-900px widths.
findings.fix: Either measure `.canvas-tools`'s actual rendered height at runtime and position the align-bar below it (e.g. via a CSS custom property set from JS, or `position:sticky` inside the flow instead of a magic-number `top`), or add a toolbar breakpoint that keeps `.canvas-tools` to one line down to the width where `.grid3` collapses.

findings.issue: The new autosave-pill dot reuses the existing generic `.dot` class (already used for catalog category-color swatches) — `<i class="dot">` inside `#save-status` — relying on the more-specific `.save-status .dot{...}` rule to override the generic `.dot{width:8px;height:8px;border-radius:2px}` rule. It works correctly today because of selector specificity, but it's a fragile naming collision that a future edit to either rule could silently break.
findings.fix: Rename the status pill's indicator class to something unambiguous, e.g. `.save-status .status-dot`, to avoid relying on specificity ordering against an unrelated existing `.dot` class.

result.integrationReport:
Integration pass complete. Everything is clean — no conflicts between the two bundles.

## `npm test`
**46 tests, 46 pass, 0 fail** (up from the 40 baseline; the two bundles added 6 — the new geometry helpers `tilesInRect`/`alignTiles`/`distributeTiles`/`rotateGroup` and the `grid.test.mjs` end-to-end `createGrid` suite covering place/undo/redo/group/align/copy/paste/duplicate/delete).

## `node --check --input-type=module` — every module
All 10 pass: `app.js, catalog.js, data.js, geometry.js, grid.js, schematic.js, storage.js, summary.js, units.js, util.js` — all **OK**.

## Features shipped in this wave

**Interaction-core (js/grid.js + geometry.js)**
- **Undo / redo** — `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Shift+Z` (and `Ctrl+Y`); toolbar `↶`/`↷`; 60-step history with arrow-nudge coalescing. History dropdown (`🕘`) jumps to any state.
- **Multi-select** — Shift/Ctrl-click to add/remove; drag on empty canvas = rubber-band marquee; `Ctrl+A` select all; `Esc` clear.
- **Groups** — `Ctrl+G` group / `Ctrl+Shift+G` ungroup (also on the align bar `⛓`/`⛓⃠`); group members move/rotate/select as one.
- **Copy / paste / duplicate** — `Ctrl+C`/`Ctrl+V` (cascade-offset), `Ctrl+D` duplicate, Alt-drag to drag fresh copies.
- **Align / distribute** — floating align bar (appears at 2+ selection): 6 align modes, H/V distribute (3+).

**Quick-win polish**
- **Autosave indicator** — `#save-status` pill flips to "Saving…" during the 600ms debounce, back to "All changes saved".
- **First-run empty state** — 3-step guide + "Load a sample city"; dismissed by ✕, sample load, or first tile placed; session-scoped (`bcp.firstRunSeen`).
- **Grip affordance** — one-time `grip-intro` pulse on the resize corner the first time content renders.
- **Shortcuts modal** — `?` or the `?` toolbar button; `Esc` closes.
- **Interaction juice** — tile `pop` entrance, drag-lift, delete ghosts, free-rotate 1:1 tracking (`.rotating` suppresses the elastic tween).
- **Focus ring** — high-contrast `:focus-visible` everywhere + a tile "halo" variant.

## Integration verification
- **No shortcut collisions**: grid's `window` keydown gates on Ctrl/Meta (Z/Y/C/V/D/G/A); app's `document` keydown owns only `?` and modal-`Esc`; board's `Esc`/`R`/arrows/Delete fire only when a tile is focused. No key is double-bound.
- **Shortcuts modal is accurate and complete** — every row (nudge, R, Delete, Esc, Ctrl+A, undo/redo, Ctrl+Y, copy/paste, duplicate, group/ungroup, `?`) maps to a real binding; every gesture row matches grid.js.
- **No duplicate CSS selectors / conflict markers**; all classes referenced by JS (`save-status`, `first-run`, `modal-backdrop`, `align-bar`, `sel-box`, `marquee`, `tile-ghost`, `grip.intro`, `tile.dragging/.rotating`) exist.
- **Pop-keyframe gotcha respected** — `.tile { animation:pop .38s … }` has **no** fill-mode, so control reverts to the inline `transform` after entrance; the keyframe reads `rotate(var(--rot))` rather than a literal angle, so a tile's rotation is never frozen wrong. The public `createGrid` API is intact and additive.

## Caveats before browser-testing / deploy
- **Minor deviation from the literal "pop must stay fade-only" note**: the `pop` keyframe animates `transform: scale(...)` (an overshoot bounce), not opacity alone. This is intentional and *safe* — it preserves rotation via `var(--rot)` and uses no fill-mode — but it does animate transform, so if you ever add `fill:both`/`forwards` to that rule it will resurface the rotation-override bug. Leave the fill-mode off.
- `git status` shows `test/grid.test.mjs` is untracked (new file) and `css/styles.css, index.html, js/app.js, js/geometry.js, js/grid.js, test/geometry.test.mjs` modified — nothing else touched. No commit/deploy performed, as instructed.
- All motion helpers guard `requestAnimationFrame`/`matchMedia` absence and honor `prefers-reduced-motion`, so the node test DOM mock and reduced-motion users are covered.
